title: One-Shot Bodies, Streaming, Uploads, Decompression, and Resource Limits · Browser and Node Client… · Contract Lens: Bounded Response Reads
summary: A media uploader streams files and reads large responses through Web Streams without accidental cloning, locked bodies, or unbounded decoding. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts bounded response reads increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch bounded streaming body pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Limit compressed and decoded response reads before allocation, parsing, logging, or persistence. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Treat request and response bodies as owned one-shot streams with explicit read, close, replay, and cleanup rules. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
